title: Methods and apparatus for facilitating multi trp uplink communication
abstract: A method, for a user equipment (UE) configured to perform multi transmission and reception point (TRP) uplink (UL) communications with a first TRP and a second TRP, comprises: receiving, from the first TRP and/or the second TRP, configuration information usable to determine, when a signalling timing difference between the first TRP and the second TRP is greater than a signalling timing difference threshold, which one of the first TRP and the second TRP to stop uplink communication with and/or which one of the first TRP and the second TRP to continue uplink communication with; receiving, from the first TRP, a first signal; receiving, from the second TRP, a second signal; and in response to determining, based on the first signal and the second signal, that the signalling timing difference between the first TRP and the second TRP is greater than the signalling timing difference threshold: determining, based on the configuration information, which one of the first TRP and the second TRP to stop uplink communication with and/or which one of the first TRP and the second TRP to continue uplink communication with; and responsively: stopping uplink communication with one of the first TRP and the second TRP.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. An apparatus configured to perform multi transmission and reception point (TRP) uplink (UL) communications with a first TRP and a second TRP, the apparatus comprising: at least one processor; at least one memory storing instructions, wherein the instructions, when executed by the at least one processor, cause the apparatus to perform, at least: receiving, from the first TRP and/or the second TRP, configuration information, the configuration information being usable to determine, when a signalling timing difference between the first TRP and the second TRP is greater than a signalling timing difference threshold, which one of the first TRP and the second TRP to stop uplink communication with and/or which one of the first TRP and the second TRP to continue uplink communication with; receiving, from the first TRP, a first signal; receiving, from the second TRP, a second signal; and in response to determining, based on the first signal and the second signal, that the signalling timing difference between the first TRP and the second TRP is greater than the signalling timing difference threshold: determining, based on the configuration information, which one of the first TRP and the second TRP to stop uplink communication with and/or which one of the first TRP and the second TRP to continue uplink communication with; and responsively: stopping uplink communication with one of the first TRP and the second TRP. 2. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the instructions, when executed by the at least one processor, cause the apparatus to further perform: performing single TRP uplink communication with the other one of the first TRP and the second TRP. 3. The apparatus of claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the instructions, when executed by the at least one processor, cause the apparatus to further perform: determining, based on the first signal and the second signal, a current receive timing difference (RTD); and 

 wherein determining that the signalling timing difference between the first TRP and the second TRP is greater than the signalling timing difference threshold comprises: determining whether the current RTD is greater than a maximum RTD (MRTD). 4. The apparatus of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the instructions, when executed by the at least one processor, cause the apparatus to further perform: determining, based on the first signal and the second signal, a current RTD; determining, based on a message received via the first TRP, a first timing advance (TA) associated with the first TRP; determining, based on a message received via the second TRP, a second TA associated with the second TRP; and determining, based at least on the current RTD, the first TA, and the second TA, a current transmit timing difference (TTD); wherein determining that the signalling timing difference between the first TRP and the second TRP is greater than the signalling timing difference threshold comprises: determining whether the current TTD is greater than a maximum TTD (MTTD). 5. The apparatus of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the configuration information is received in the form of a radio resource control (RRC) message. 6. The apparatus of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the instructions, when executed by the at least one processor, cause the apparatus to further perform: receiving, from the first TRP and/or the second TRP, updated configuration information; and overwriting the configuration information based on the updated received configuration information. 7. The apparatus of any one of the preceding claims, wherein stopping uplink communication with one of the first TRP and the second TRP comprises applying a transmission configuration indicator (TCI) state associated with the other one of the first TRP and the second TRP. 8. The apparatus of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the configuration information is indicative of one or more selection criteria, wherein determining which one of the first TRP and the second TRP to stop uplink communication with and/or which one of the first TRP and the second TRP to continue uplink communication with is based on determining that one of the first TRP and the second TRP satisfies the one or more selection criteria. 9. The apparatus of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the configuration information is indicative of a particular TRP of the first TRP and the second TRP to be kept and/or wherein the configuration information is indicative of a particular TRP of the first TRP and the second TRP to be dropped. 10. The apparatus of claim 9, wherein the configuration information indicates a specific TCI state to keep in case the signalling timing difference exceeds a signalling timing difference threshold. 11. The apparatus of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the instructions, when executed by the at least one processor, cause the apparatus to further perform: determining that the apparatus is configured to keep a single UL connection when the signalling time difference exceeds the signalling time difference threshold based on a configuration setting associated with keeping a single UL connection when the signalling time difference exceeds the signalling time difference threshold value being set to a particular value; and determining that the apparatus is currently operating in a multi TRP mode, wherein determining that the apparatus is currently operating in a multi TRP mode comprises: determining that two TCI states are activated for control resource set (CORESET) by an activation command and/or that the two TCI states are associated with different index values. 12. An apparatus comprising: at least one processor; at least one memory storing instructions, wherein the instructions, when executed by the at least one processor, cause the apparatus to perform, at least: sending, to a user equipment, UE, configured for multi TRP communication with a first TRP and a second TRP, configuration information, the configuration information being usable by the UE to determine, when a 

 signalling timing difference between the first TRP and the second TRP is greater than a signalling timing difference threshold, which one of the first TRP and the second TRP to stop uplink communication with and/or which one of the first TRP and the second TRP to continue uplink communication with; and receiving, via the one of the first TRP and the second TRP with which the UE has continued uplink communication based on the configuration information, a single-TRP uplink communication from the UE. 13. The apparatus of claim 12, wherein the instructions, when executed by the at least one processor, cause the apparatus to further perform: stopping receiving, via the one of the first TRP and the second TRP with which the UE has stopped uplink communication based on the configuration information, uplink communication from the UE. 14. The apparatus of claim 12 or claim 13, wherein the signalling timing difference between the first TRP and the second TRP is greater than the signalling timing difference threshold when a RTD is greater than a MRTD and/or a TTD is greater than a MTTD. 15. The apparatus of any one of claims 12 to 14, wherein the configuration information is sent in the form of a RRC message. 16. The apparatus of any one of claims 12 to 15, wherein the configuration information is sent in response to the UE being configured to perform multi TRP UL communications with the first TRP and the second TRP. 17. The apparatus of any one of claims 12 to 16, wherein the instructions, when executed by the at least one processor, cause the apparatus to further perform: sending, to the UE, updated configuration information to cause the UE to overwrite the configuration information based on the updated received configuration information. 18. The apparatus of any one of claims 12 to 17 wherein the instructions, when executed by the at least one processor, cause the apparatus to further perform: sending, to another UE configured for multi TRP UL communications, different configuration information, the different configuration information being different to the configuration information. 19. The apparatus of any one of claims 12 to 18, wherein stopping uplink communication with the one of the first TRP and the second TRP comprises applying a transmission configuration indicator (TCI) state associated with the other one of the first TRP and the second TRP. 20. The apparatus of any one of claims 12 to 19, wherein the configuration information is indicative of one or more selection criteria, wherein determining which one of the first TRP and the second TRP to stop uplink communication with and/or which one of the first TRP and the second TRP to continue uplink communication with is based on determining that the selected one of the first TRP and the second TRP satisfies the one or more selection criteria. 21. The apparatus of any one of claims 12 to 20, wherein the configuration information is indicative of a particular TRP of the first TRP and the second TRP to be kept and/or a particular TRP of the first TRP and the second TRP to be dropped. 22. The apparatus of claim 21, wherein the configuration information indicates a specific TCI state to keep in case the signalling timing difference exceeds the signalling timing difference threshold. 23. The apparatus of any one of claims 12 to 22, wherein the instructions, when executed by the at least one processor, cause the apparatus to further perform: determining that the UE is configured to keep a single UL connection when the signalling time difference exceeds the signalling time difference threshold based on a configuration setting associated with keeping a single UL connection when the signalling time difference exceeds the signalling time difference threshold value being set to a particular value; and determining that the UE is currently operating in a multi TRP mode, wherein determining that the UE is currently operating in a multi TRP mode comprises: determining that two TCI states are activated for control resource set (CORESET) by an activation command and/or that the two TCI states are associated with different index values. 24. A method performed by a user equipment (UE) configured to perform multi transmission and reception point (TRP) uplink (UL) communications with a first TRP and a second TRP, comprising: 

 receiving, from the first TRP and/or the second TRP, configuration information, the configuration information being usable to determine, when a signalling timing difference between the first TRP and the second TRP is greater than a signalling timing difference threshold, which one of the first TRP and the second TRP to stop uplink communication with and/or which one of the first TRP and the second TRP to continue uplink communication with; receiving, from the first TRP, a first signal; receiving, from the second TRP, a second signal; and in response to determining, based on the first signal and the second signal, that the signalling timing difference between the first TRP and the second TRP is greater than the signalling timing difference threshold: determining, based on the configuration information, which one of the first TRP and the second TRP to stop uplink communication with and/or which one of the first TRP and the second TRP to continue uplink communication with; and responsively: stopping uplink communication with one of the first TRP and the second TRP. 25. A method performed by a radio base station, comprising: sending, to a user equipment, UE, configured for multi TRP communication with a first TRP and a second TRP, configuration information, the configuration information being usable by the UE to determine, when a signalling timing difference between the first TRP and the second TRP is greater than a signalling timing difference threshold, which one of the first TRP and the second TRP to stop uplink communication with and/or which one of the first TRP and the second TRP to continue uplink communication with; and receiving, via the one of the first TRP and the second TRP with which the UE has continued uplink communication based on the configuration information, a single- TRP uplink communication from the UE. 26. An apparatus, for a user equipment (UE) configured to perform multi transmission and reception point (TRP) uplink (UL) communications with a first TRP and a second TRP, comprising: mean for receiving, from the first TRP and/or the second TRP, configuration information, the configuration information being usable to determine, when a signalling timing difference between the first TRP and the second TRP is greater than a signalling timing difference threshold, which one of the first TRP and the 

second TRP to stop uplink communication with and/or which one of the first TRP and the second TRP to continue uplink communication with; means for receiving, from the first TRP, a first signal; means for receiving, from the second TRP, a second signal; and means for, in response to determining, based on the first signal and the second signal, that the signalling timing difference between the first TRP and the second TRP is greater than the signalling timing difference threshold: determining, based on the configuration information, which one of the first TRP and the second TRP to stop uplink communication with and/or which one of the first TRP and the second TRP to continue uplink communication with; and responsively: stopping uplink communication with one of the first TRP and the second TRP. 27. An apparatus, for a radio base station, comprising: means for sending, to a user equipment, UE, configured for multi TRP communication with a first TRP and a second TRP, configuration information, the configuration information being usable by the UE to determine, when a signalling timing difference between the first TRP and the second TRP is greater than a signalling timing difference threshold, which one of the first TRP and the second TRP to stop uplink communication with and/or which one of the first TRP and the second TRP to continue uplink communication with; and means for receiving, via the one of the first TRP and the second TRP with which the UE has continued uplink communication based on the configuration information, a single-TRP uplink communication from the UE. 28. A computer program comprising instructions which, when executed by an apparatus, cause the apparatus to perform the method of claim 24 or claim 25.
